revisionReason: Restructured to match client's Master Outline (MentaLIFE Wellness Course Master Outline.docx). Zones 2/3 swapped (Mindset → Zone 2, Stress → Zone 3). Zone 1 expanded with Mental Health Continuum + stress intro. Three outcome videos merged into one.

designPhilosophy.metaphor: The course IS the mental health continuum — a living landscape that shifts from gray survival to vibrant thriving as the learner progresses
designPhilosophy.learnerRole: A corporate professional reinforcing wellness concepts from a live workshop
designPhilosophy.courseRole: Workshop supplement — reinforces key concepts, provides practical tools, and adds reflection depth beyond the live experience

progressionSystem.visualEffect: Page color-grading warms with progression — desaturated cool tones evolve to saturated warm sage and dusty rose

jobRoles: Working professionals in corporate wellness programs
Conference attendees who saw Kelvin's live workshop
targetAudience.priorKnowledge: Attendees have seen Kelvin's live workshop overview — this course goes deeper on each topic

courseStructure.title: MentaLIFE: A Framework for Living a Happy, Healthy, High-Quality Life
courseStructure.description: A concise, video-driven wellness course that reinforces the MentaLIFE framework through short concept videos, practical reference tools, and guided reflection — designed as a companion to Kelvin Pruitt's live workshops.
learningOutcomes: Explain the three MentaLIFE outcomes (Happiness, Healthiness, High-Quality Living) and the three core pillars (Mindset Growth, Stress Management, Healthy Habits & Routines)
Describe the mental health continuum and identify where you currently exist on it
Differentiate between real and perceived stressors and understand their impact on the body
Apply mindset growth strategies including the 4-step Mindset Swap technique
Identify self-inflicted stress patterns and apply relief strategies and foundational tools
Design a personalized daily intentional living plan incorporating mindset habits, stress tools, and routine anchors

modules.title: The MentaLIFE Framework Introduction
modules.colorTemperature: cool mist — dusty blue tones, contemplative stillness
modules.description: Meet the MentaLIFE framework, understand the three outcomes, explore the mental health continuum, and learn how stress works in your body and mind.
learningOutcomes: Describe the MentaLIFE mission and the three core pillars
Define happiness, healthiness, and high-quality living as intentional outcomes
Explain the mental health continuum and identify your current position
Describe the stress response system and understand why perception matters
Differentiate between real and perceived stressors

lessons.title: Introduction to the MentaLIFE Framework
objectives: Explain the MentaLIFE mission: guiding people toward happy, healthy, high-quality living
Identify the three core pillars: Mindset Growth, Stress Management, Healthy Habits & Routines
Recognize the role of journaling and reflection in the wellness journey
suggestedActivities: Guided reflection: Three Questions to Start Your Journey
Journaling prompt: Which pillar needs strengthening and why

contentBlocks.title: Introduction to the MentaLIFE Framework
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia avatar video. Introduces mission, three pillars, reflection questions, journaling encouragement.

contentBlocks.title: Three Questions to Start Your Journey
contentBlocks.designNotes: From video: Do I believe I can achieve mental wellness? Am I being intentional about building it? Which pillar do I need to strengthen most?

lessons.title: MentaLIFE Outcomes: Happiness, Health & High-Quality Living
objectives: Define happiness as a sustained emotional state built through intention
Recognize that mental, emotional, and physical well-being are interconnected
Define high-quality living as the result of intentional daily choices
Rate your current level of happiness, health, and quality of life
suggestedActivities: Self-assessment: Rate current happiness, health, and quality of life (1-10)
Reflection: Which MentaLIFE outcome needs the most attention right now

contentBlocks.title: MentaLIFE Outcomes
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video covering all three outcomes: Happiness, Healthiness, and High-Quality Living in one unified presentation.

contentBlocks.title: Rate Your Current Well-Being
contentBlocks.designNotes: Self-assessment: Rate your current happiness, health, and quality of life on a scale of 1-10. Which area needs the most attention?

lessons.title: Mental Wellness & The Mental Health Continuum
objectives: Define mental wellness as the foundation of overall well-being
Describe the mental health continuum from surviving to thriving
Identify where you currently exist on the mental health continuum
suggestedActivities: Pause & Reflect: Place yourself on the mental health continuum
Journaling: What would it take to move one step forward

contentBlocks.title: The Mental Health Continuum
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Introduces mental wellness concept and the continuum model from surviving through thriving.

contentBlocks.title: Where Do You Exist on the Continuum?
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflect: Where do you generally exist on the mental health continuum? What would it take to move one step forward?

lessons.title: The Science and Impacts of Stress
lessons.videoScript: Zone 1 — The Science and Impacts of Stress
objectives: Describe the stress response system (perceived threat → hormones → fight/flee/freeze)
Understand why unmanaged chronic stress erodes well-being
Recognize how stress shows up in the workplace
suggestedActivities: Reflection: How is stress currently showing up in your life and workplace
Knowledge check: Match stress response stages to their effects

contentBlocks.title: The Science and Impacts of Stress
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Stress biology, perception, chronic activation danger, workplace implications.

contentBlocks.title: How Is Stress Showing Up in Your Life?
contentBlocks.designNotes: Context: Stress in the Workplace section + reflection on personal stress impact.

lessons.title: Real vs. Perceived Stress
objectives: Differentiate between real stressors and perceived stressors
Identify examples of real and perceived stressors in your own life
Recognize that perception — shaped by mindset — determines your stress experience
suggestedActivities: Self-reflection: Identify one real and one perceived stressor in your life
Wellness Check: Zone 1 knowledge assessment (6 questions)

contentBlocks.title: Real vs. Perceived Stress
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Real vs perceived stress, examples, the 90/10 rule.

contentBlocks.title: Identifying Your Stressors
contentBlocks.designNotes: Self-reflection: Identify one real stressor and one perceived stressor you are currently experiencing. How does your perception shape your reaction?

zoneAssessment.title: Wellness Check: The Framework
zoneAssessment.designNotes: Concept check: three pillars, outcomes, mental health continuum placement, stress basics, real vs perceived. 3 minutes.

modules.title: MentaLIFE Pillar #1: Mindset Growth
modules.colorTemperature: transitional — sage emerging through the mist
modules.description: Discover how your mindset shapes every aspect of your life. Learn the difference between growth and fixed mindsets, understand neuroplasticity, and practice practical strategies for rewiring your thinking.
learningOutcomes: Define mindset and micro-mindsets and their role in daily life
Identify toxic mindsets and understand their origins
Differentiate between growth and fixed mindsets using Carol Dweck's framework
Apply the Mindset Swap technique and daily mindset growth tools

lessons.title: The Power of Mindset
objectives: Define mindset as beliefs, attitudes, and assumptions that shape how you live
Recognize micro-mindsets and how they influence specific situations
Understand that neuroplasticity means your mindset can change
Identify toxic mindsets in your own life
suggestedActivities: Pause & Reflect: Identify toxic mindsets influencing your decisions
Journaling: Trace the origins of your limiting beliefs

contentBlocks.title: The Power of Mindset
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Mindset definition, micro-mindsets, positive vs negative, neuroplasticity.

contentBlocks.title: Identifying Toxic Mindsets
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflection: What toxic or limiting mindsets have been influencing your decisions? Where did they come from?

lessons.title: Mindset Growth Strategies
objectives: Differentiate between growth and fixed mindsets
Apply the 4-step Mindset Swap technique to reframe a limiting belief
Identify at least 3 daily mindset growth tools to practice
Select specific strategies to commit to this week
suggestedActivities: Interactive timeline: 4-Step Mindset Swap technique walkthrough
Interactive accordion: 10 Daily Mindset Growth Tools
Self-reflection: Select 2-3 strategies to commit to this week
Wellness Check: Zone 2 knowledge assessment (6 questions)

contentBlocks.title: Mindset Growth Strategies
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Growth vs fixed mindset, Carol Dweck, strategy descriptions.

contentBlocks.title: Selecting Strategies to Practice
contentBlocks.designNotes: Self-Reflection: From the strategies presented, which 2-3 will you commit to practicing this week? What growth belief will replace your biggest limiting belief?

zoneAssessment.title: Wellness Check: Mindset Growth
zoneAssessment.designNotes: Scenario-based: 'Your colleague says I'm just not a numbers person. Using the Mindset Swap, what should they do first?' Application-level. 3 minutes.

modules.title: Stress Management
modules.description: Identify self-inflicted stress patterns, learn foundational tools for prevention, and practice immediate deactivation techniques to regain control.
learningOutcomes: Identify patterns of self-inflicted stress and understand how to break them
Apply foundational stress prevention tools
Practice immediate stress deactivation techniques
Explain how stress management supports all three MentaLIFE outcomes

lessons.title: Decreasing Self-Inflicted Stress
objectives: Identify patterns of self-inflicted stress in your own life
Understand how overcommitting, perfectionism, and poor boundaries create stress
Recognize the difference between stress you can control and stress you create
suggestedActivities: Pause & Reflect: Identify which self-inflicted stress pattern resonates most
Journaling: Map your stress triggers to controllable behaviors

contentBlocks.title: Decreasing Self-Inflicted Stress
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Self-inflicted stress patterns, identification, breaking the cycle.

contentBlocks.title: Identifying Self-Inflicted Stressors
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflect: What self-inflicted stressors are you currently creating? Which pattern — overcommitting, perfectionism, poor planning, ignoring signs, or saying yes when you mean no — resonates most?

lessons.title: Stress Relief Strategies, Tools and Practices
objectives: Apply at least 3 foundational stress prevention tools
Practice immediate stress deactivation techniques
Explain how stress management supports all three MentaLIFE outcomes
Select specific strategies, tools, or practices to adopt
suggestedActivities: Interactive accordion: 9 Foundational Stress Prevention Tools
Interactive flashcards: 10 Stress Deactivation Techniques
Interactive tabs: Stress Management ↔ MentaLIFE Outcomes connection
Self-reflection: Pick 2 foundational tools and 2 techniques to adopt
Wellness Check: Zone 3 knowledge assessment (6 questions)

contentBlocks.title: Stress Relief Strategies, Tools and Practices
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Foundational tools, deactivation techniques, outcome connections.

contentBlocks.title: Identifying Strategies to Adopt
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflection: From the strategies, tools, and practices presented, which will you adopt? Pick 2 foundational tools and 2 deactivation techniques.

contentBlocks.title: Your Stress Management Commitment
contentBlocks.designNotes: Self-Reflection closing: How will you handle stress differently starting today?

zoneAssessment.title: Wellness Check: Stress Management
zoneAssessment.designNotes: Scenario-based: 'Your coworker is venting about a deadline and you feel your shoulders tighten. Which deactivation technique would help right now?' Mix of identification and application. 3 minutes.

modules.title: Healthy Routines and Habits
modules.colorTemperature: full warmth — deep sage, dusty rose accents, rich earth tones
modules.description: Build the daily life. Understand habits and routines, integrate mindset growth and stress management into a unified lifestyle, and create your personalized wellness plan.
learningOutcomes: Differentiate between habits and routines and understand the 66-day formation research
Understand how the MentaLIFE lifestyle integrates mindset growth and stress management
Design a personalized daily wellness routine incorporating MentaLIFE principles
Commit to specific intentional actions using the Daily Intentional Living Checklist

lessons.title: Building a Lifestyle through Routines and Habits
lessons.videoScript: Zone 4 — Building a Lifestyle through Routines and Habits
objectives: Differentiate between habits (automatic) and routines (structured sequences)
Understand that 45% of daily actions are habitual and the 66-day formation timeline
Recognize the lifestyle health factor and its connection to quality of life
suggestedActivities: Self-reflection: Assess current routines and identify helpful vs harmful habits
Journaling: What habits would you like to build over the next 66 days

contentBlocks.title: Building a Lifestyle through Routines and Habits
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Habits vs routines, 45% autopilot, 66-day research, lifestyle health factor.

contentBlocks.title: Routines and Habits Self-Assessment
contentBlocks.designNotes: Self Reflection: What routines do you currently follow? Which habits are working for you, and which are working against you?

lessons.title: The MentaLIFE Lifestyle
objectives: Understand how the MentaLIFE lifestyle integrates mindset growth and stress management strategies
Identify which mindset and stress management strategies you are currently using
Recognize the connection between all three pillars in building a sustainable lifestyle
suggestedActivities: Pause & Reflect: Assess which mindset and stress strategies you currently use
Integration exercise: Map how all three pillars connect in your daily life

contentBlocks.title: The MentaLIFE Lifestyle
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Integrating mindset growth and stress management strategies into daily lifestyle.

contentBlocks.title: Current Strategies Assessment
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflect: What strategies am I currently using for mindset growth and stress management? What's working? What needs to change?

lessons.title: Routines and Habits that Build Lifestyle
lessons.videoScript: Zone 4 — Routines and Habits that Build Lifestyle
objectives: Explore MentaLIFE-aligned morning and evening routines
Identify daily self-care practices across physical, mental, social, and spiritual dimensions
Build a personalized Daily Intentional Living plan
Commit to patience, discipline, consistency, and self-accountability
suggestedActivities: Interactive accordion: MentaLIFE-aligned daily routines
Interactive tabs: Outcomes in Practice (Happiness, Healthiness, HQL)
Interactive scenario: Daily Intentional Living Planner
Course self-reflection: Commit to one intentional action this week
Wellness Check: Zone 4 knowledge assessment (6 questions)

contentBlocks.title: Routines and Habits that Build Lifestyle
contentBlocks.designNotes: Client-provided Synthesia video. Routine/habit descriptions, practical tools, daily intentional living.

contentBlocks.title: Your Wellness Lifestyle Plan
contentBlocks.designNotes: Pause & Reflect: Identify strategies, tools and practices that support Mindset Growth, Stress Management and include routines and habits to create a WELLNESS LIFESTYLE.

contentBlocks.title: Course Self-Reflection
contentBlocks.designNotes: Final course self-reflection: What is one intentional action you'll take this week? What routine will you build first? Who will you share this commitment with?

zoneAssessment.title: Wellness Check: Healthy Routines & Habits
zoneAssessment.designNotes: Application: 'You want to improve your fitness but keep skipping workouts. Using MentaLIFE principles, what's the first step?' Mix of habits/routines concepts and daily planning. 3 minutes.

metadata.revisionTrigger: Client Master Outline — MentaLIFE Wellness Course Master Outline.docx
notes: Restructured from v3's 13 lessons (4+3+3+3) to 12 lessons (5+2+2+3) per Master Outline
Zone 2 is now Mindset Growth (Pillar #1), Zone 3 is Stress Management — swapped from v3
Zone 1 expanded: includes Mental Health Continuum + Stress intro (Understanding + Real vs Perceived)
Three separate outcome videos (Happiness, Healthiness, HQL) merged into one lesson
New lesson: Mental Wellness & The Mental Health Continuum (lesson-1-3)
New lesson: The MentaLIFE Lifestyle (lesson-4-2) — integrates all pillars into lifestyle
Videos are client-provided Synthesia avatars — NOT generated by the platform
Workshop-support positioning: reinforces concepts, doesn't teach from scratch
Three core pillars: Mindset Growth, Stress Management, Healthy Habits & Routines
Three outcomes: Happiness, Healthiness, High-Quality Living
Zone order per Master Outline: Framework → Mindset → Stress → Habits
Continuum progression system retained from v2/v3
Kelvin has a conference April 21 — soft delivery target
